_comment: Example project config for NTE trade policy classification. Shows how to fill in the generic template for a trade barrier extraction project.

inclusion_criteria.actor: A foreign government maintaining the barrier (not the US or document author)
inclusion_criteria.action: A trade barrier, discriminatory regulation, subsidy, or restrictive practice that affects trade flows, market access, or competitive conditions
inclusion_criteria.scope: Current barriers only — still in force. Exclude barriers that have been fully removed. Exception: include if the document complains that a reform did not go far enough (the remaining restriction is the barrier).

exclusion_criteria: Actions by the document's author (retaliatory tariffs, sanctions, treaty withdrawals, dispute initiations)
Purely domestic policies with no trade dimension
Passages that merely mention a keyword without describing an actual barrier
Liberalisations — barriers that have been removed, reduced, or reformed (unless reform was insufficient)
unit_of_analysis: One distinct trade concern or government policy. Multiple quotes about the same policy = one entry. Different aspects of one law = one entry. Different policies or sectors = separate entries.

output_fields._comment: Trade-policy-specific fields beyond the core set.

custom_fields.name: acting_government
custom_fields.description: The foreign government maintaining this barrier

audit_dimensions.category_match: Does this entry fall within the theme's specific domain? Product-specific themes require product-specific barriers.
audit_dimensions.jurisdiction: Is the barrier maintained by the foreign government, not the document's author?
audit_dimensions.trade_framing: Does this concern affect trade flows, market access, or competitive conditions?
audit_dimensions.currency: Is the barrier still in force? Has it been removed or reformed?
audit_dimensions.quote_fidelity: Is the exact_quote truly verbatim from the source document?

_taxonomy_note: The MAST taxonomy reference (in examples/ directory) defines intervention type classifications.
